core_purpose: Runtime Topology Intelligence = visibility layer for legitimacy-relevant state. Not: authority layer, execution layer, reconciliation engine, validator, or proof generator.
core_invariant: Only observations capable of altering distributed legitimacy interpretation belong in Runtime Topology Intelligence.

boundary_rules.observer_responsibility: observe -> compare -> classify visibility -> emit only

categories.description: Local process metrics with no legitimacy implication.
Lifecycle events that affect governance interpretation.
Events that can directly affect reconciliation outcome or closure.
Events in the authority lifecycle observable without creating authority.
Events that affect replay safety or nonce ledger state.
Events in the proof lifecycle observable without creating proof.
Events that cannot affect execution eligibility, authority interpretation, replay safety, proof binding, or reconciliation outcome.
categories.selection_rule: If an observation cannot materially affect execution eligibility, authority interpretation, replay safety, proof binding, or reconciliation outcome, classify as TOPOLOGY_NOISE.

observation_selection_rule.observe: anything that changes lineage, authority, proof, replay, reconciliation, or governance interpretation
observation_selection_rule.ignore: anything that only changes performance, capacity, throughput, resource utilization, or local process noise

observation_taxonomy.primary_legitimacy_relevance_test: Can this observation materially affect execution eligibility, authority interpretation, replay safety, proof binding, or reconciliation outcome? If no: TOPOLOGY_NOISE.

elevation_rules.description: Operational events are elevated only when they impair legitimacy visibility.

examples.reason: Container restart alone does not impair legitimacy visibility.

examples.event: Validator unavailable for 17 minutes + registry observations missing + proof emissions not observed
examples.reason: Combination impairs the system's ability to observe legitimacy-relevant state.

elevation_rules.core_rule: Events are not elevated because they happened. Events are elevated because they changed the system's ability to observe legitimacy-relevant state.

anchor_questions_every_observation_must_: From whose topology view was it observed?

typed_evidence_envelope.description: Avoid free-form observation blobs. Use typed evidence envelopes.

evidence.evidence_type: REGISTRY_STATE | PROOF_STATE | REPLAY_STATE | AUTHORITY_STATE | TOPOLOGY_GAP | GOVERNANCE_STATE

typed_evidence_envelope.core_rule: Summary describes evidence. It must not interpret legitimacy.

observation_propagation_constraints.rule: No observation may be propagated without classification metadata.

stability_window_and_flapping_model.stability_confirmed_transition_rule: A transition becomes emission-eligible only if: (1) it persists past a stability window, OR (2) it affects a critical legitimacy surface, OR (3) it creates an irreversible lineage transition.

safe_behavior.if_bridgeable_independent_evidence_exist: reconciliation may proceed using lineage-bound evidence

reconciliation_safety_rule.core_rule: Flapping topology cannot produce closure. Only stable topology or bridgeable lineage-bound evidence can support reconciliation.

states.description: All required topology surfaces are observable and consistent.
Some topology surfaces are observable; coverage is incomplete.
Topology observations exist but exceed acceptable staleness threshold.
Conflicting observations exist for the same topology surface.
Required topology surfaces cannot be observed.
Observation classified as TOPOLOGY_NOISE and suppressed.

topology_closure_states.core_boundary: Topology observer may classify visibility state. Topology observer may not classify legitimacy state.

classifications.condition: Convergent anchor — all lineage-bound anchors converge on the same lineage.
Missing anchor — required lineage anchor is absent.
Conflicting anchor — lineage anchors contradict each other.

gap_bridgeability_semantics.replay_gap_bridgeable_only_when: nonce, execution, proof, and object hash all converge on the same lineage.

proof_gap_examples.scenario: proof entry exists but validated_object_hash differs from execution record
proof lineage converges on same execution_id and validated_object_hash

gap_bridgeability_semantics.closure_effect: UNKNOWN or UNBRIDGEABLE -> no reconciliation closure

tiers.label: Verified immutable lineage anchors
tiers.description: Cryptographically verified, append-only ledger entries with confirmed integrity.

tiers.label: Signed registry state
tiers.description: Registry entries with verified signatures and lineage binding.

tiers.label: Topology observations
tiers.description: Runtime topology intelligence observations classified per this taxonomy.

tiers.label: Derived reconstruction
tiers.description: State derived from partial evidence when direct observation is unavailable.

integrity_suspension_rule.if_tier1_integrity_verifies: Tier 1 wins. Tier 2 becomes registry drift.
integrity_suspension_rule.if_tier1_integrity_suspect: Do not fall back to mutable registry truth. Suspend closure.
integrity_suspension_rule.core_compression: Ledger finality wins only when ledger integrity verifies. If ledger integrity fails, the system suspends closure.

#1641.scope: Observation metadata schema and typed evidence envelope implementation.

#1642.scope: Emission trigger model and selective emission logic.

#1644.scope: Evidence precedence hierarchy and conflict arbitration.
